cover_system: notebook or white-paper base
handwritten black headline with red and blue emphasis
yellow highlighter, arrows, circles, stars, and small diagrams
one lesson or promise per tile
occasional dark expert tile creates contrast without replacing the main visual grammar
project_value: Reference for a warm educational account that feels authored by a person rather than generated by a dashboard.
Useful alternative first-screen grammar for guides, operating notes, and prompt collections.
items.guardrail: Follower and growth claims visible in the grid are source claims, not verified performance evidence.

items.first_screen_impression: AI automation education mixed with a recurring AI or virtual female presenter
cover_system: alternates white information cards, dark automation diagrams, and presenter-led videos
presenter close-ups provide human continuity
bold subtitles state provocative identity or automation claims
tool logos and result numbers act as fast context
project_value: Reference for combining an educational product with a recurring presenter identity.
Shows the risk of a first screen feeling split when information cards and presenter videos do not share enough typography or color logic.
Generated-presenter disclosures can itself become a narrative hook.
items.guardrail: This screenshot does not prove persistent avatar identity, production quality, automation savings, or tool connectivity.

items.first_screen_impression: technical AI-video production and tool demonstration channel
cover_system: dark sample footage or software screen as the base
large white headline with yellow result or tool phrase
tool logos placed near the top
comparison labels such as image versus prompt or 4K versus 1080p
real generated output remains visible behind the promise
project_value: Reference for a future Shorts production knowledge stream.
Good model for pairing a tool claim with visible output, comparison, or workflow evidence on the cover.
items.guardrail: Visible service, resolution, access, and tool claims remain unverified reference material.

items.first_screen_impression: emotion-first movie and drama highlight stream
cover_system: one- or two-line white hook at the very top
tight expressive face or high-tension scene crop
original dialogue subtitle remains inside the footage
small program or platform badge supplies context
black bands and consistent placement unify different titles
items.project_value: Reference for story and entertainment accounts where the opening emotion matters more than decorative graphic design.

visible_claims: a profile visit after viewing content may signal account interest and increase later exposure even without a follow
uploads through Edits may be exposed in an Edits Reels tab and their views may count toward Instagram views
new features may receive early platform attention
project_value: Treat profile entry as an intermediate funnel event between content view and follow.
Design the first nine tiles and pinned posts to convert profile visits into deeper viewing.
Record publishing path as an experiment dimension if an approved Edits workflow is later available.
items.guardrail: These are platform-strategy claims from a screenshot and must be verified before becoming an operating rule.

items.first_screen_impression: compact, high-alert crime, money, and daily-issue video news
cover_system: black account and grid background
white setup headline with a magenta boxed consequence or quote
small source footage centered below the headline
tiny brand mark repeated at the top
source or reenactment context placed vertically near the edge
one short issue per tile with minimal decoration
items.editorial_positioning: 범죄·돈·일상 이슈를 쟁점만 한 입 사이즈로; images are staged for context.
project_value: Strong first-screen reference for concise issue video while preserving a visible distinction between reporting and contextual reenactment.
The magenta quote block creates a consistent account signal without forcing one type of source image.
items.metric_observation: Visible views vary substantially across tiles; this supports topic-by-topic analysis but does not prove a universal cover winner.

visible_roles.purpose: find and resolve why a site is not appearing in search
optimize for AI-assisted and conversational search
design navigable site and menu structure
generate structured search pages at scale

items.design_learning: Warm off-white background, category tabs, role cards, persistent icon, short purpose line, and large sequence number make a complex system scannable.
items.project_value: Potential future checklist and role registry for discoverability work; not proof that these skills are installed.

visible_roles.purpose: repair weak pages to increase conversion
reduce abandonment through signup and trial
help a new user reach first value quickly
reduce inquiry and application form abandonment
use an exit moment for a relevant intervention

project_value: Useful reference for the future operator dashboard and approval UX, especially onboarding, forms, and upgrade clarity.
Each role should become a measurable check rather than a decorative agent name.

visible_roles.purpose: operate Google, Meta, and LinkedIn advertising
design a controlled A versus B comparison
collect click and conversion data without gaps

project_value: Correct sequence is creative hypothesis, controlled test, reliable tracking, then decision.
A paid-media lane remains an approval-gated future capability, not a current project connection.

visible_roles.purpose: create a useful free tool that attracts qualified users
identify and reduce reasons users leave

project_value: Useful later for audience growth beyond paid distribution.
A free-tool strategy could package a bounded useful part of the system, while referral and retention require real user evidence first.

cross_batch_learning: Account first screens should communicate the content promise before an individual post is opened.
Handwritten education, presenter-led automation, technical demonstration, entertainment emotion, and compact news each need distinct grid grammar.
Profile visits can be modeled as a funnel step, but platform distribution claims require verification.
A marketing operating system becomes understandable when broad departments are decomposed into narrow measurable roles.
External role lists are reusable architecture references, not evidence that a skill is installed or useful without integration and testing.
